sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: RELX share price steadies near a 52-week low as buybacks roll on and results loom
feed.summary: RELX shares stabilized slightly after a recent dip, trading near their 52-week low, following news of ongoing share buybacks and a new AI-driven product from its LexisNexis Risk unit. Investors are keenly awaiting the annual results on February 12th for insights into the company's outlook, future buyback plans, and dividends. Despite the fresh product initiative and consistent capital returns, the company faces intense competitive pressure from rivals also rolling out AI tools.

feed.title: ADS-TEC bags supply, installation of 10-MW/20-MWh BESS in Germany
feed.summary: ADS-TEC Energy has secured a contract for the supply and installation of a 10-MW/20-MWh battery energy storage system (BESS) in Germany. The article details this development in the energy storage sector, located specifically in Germany within the European region. The news is part of broader updates in the renewable energy market.

feed.title: QUALCOMM Incorporated $QCOM Shares Sold by Arizona State Retirement System
feed.summary: Arizona State Retirement System reduced its stake in QUALCOMM by 2.3% in Q3, selling 7,294 shares, while insider selling by executives also occurred. Despite insider sales, QUALCOMM beat Q3 earnings estimates, issued strong Q1 FY2026 guidance, and announced a quarterly dividend. Analyst ratings are mixed, with a consensus "Hold" but some expressing positive sentiment on Qualcomm's push into on-device AI and cash generation.

feed.title: Global Minimum Tax And Delta Deal Reshape GE Aerospace Outlook
feed.summary: GE Aerospace is facing increased tax expenses due to Singapore's adoption of the 15% global minimum corporate tax, which has reduced its tax benefits by $121 million. Simultaneously, the company secured a significant long-term deal with Delta Air Lines to supply GEnx engines, spare parts, and services for its Boeing 787-10 fleet, creating a multi-year revenue stream. These developments present a mixed outlook for investors, with higher tax burdens potentially impacting future earnings but the Delta deal providing long-term revenue visibility and reinforcing GE Aerospace's market position.

feed.title: Apple Earnings: Prolific Quarter Portends Terrific 2026 Growth
feed.summary: Apple posted stellar December-quarter results, driven by significant iPhone revenue growth and gross margin expansion. This strong performance, especially in China, has led Morningstar to raise its fair value estimate for Apple, anticipating a robust iPhone growth cycle in fiscal 2026. The company's ability to compete and expand margins despite headwinds suggests continued positive momentum.

feed.title: $14 trillion asset manager BlackRock unveils its newest weapon in Wall Street ‘alts’ talent war: profit sharing from private markets
feed.summary: BlackRock, the world's largest asset manager, has introduced an "executive carry program" offering senior executives a share of profits from private markets funds. This initiative aims to retain top talent in the competitive alternative asset management industry, which is projected to grow significantly. The program includes stringent forfeiture provisions for executives who leave for competitors, and follows BlackRock's substantial acquisitions in the alts space to expand its offerings.

feed.title: 2 Trillion-Dollar Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stocks To Double Up on Right Now
feed.summary: The article identifies Broadcom (AVGO) and Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) as top AI semiconductor stocks for 2026, highlighting their critical roles in the expanding AI infrastructure. Broadcom provides essential networking gear and helps design custom silicon solutions, while TSMC acts as the world's largest chip manufacturer, fabricating chips for major tech companies. Both are expected to benefit significantly from the projected $500 billion infrastructure spending by AI developers this year.

feed.title: Apple, Verizon And 3 Stocks To Watch Heading Into Friday
feed.summary: This article highlights several stocks to watch on Friday, January 30, 2026, including Apple, Exxon Mobil, Verizon Communications, SanDisk Corp., and Chevron. Apple reported stronger-than-expected Q1 fiscal 2026 results, beating revenue and EPS estimates. Other companies like Exxon Mobil, Verizon, and Chevron are expected to release their quarterly earnings, while SanDisk Corp. already posted upbeat results and extended a joint venture.
